task: My mom’s been having a rough week, and I’m trying to keep up with the basics without making a mess of it. Can you help me make a simple plan for what to watch, what to write down, and when it’s time to call the doctor instead of waiting it out?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. Here’s a simple, plain plan you can use.

## **Mom Check-In Plan**

### **Watch for**
- Eating less than usual
- Drinking less than usual
- More tired, confused, or “not herself”
- New pain
- Trouble walking, standing, or getting around
- Fever or chills
- Coughing, shortness of breath, or chest pain
- Vomiting, diarrhea, or not keeping fluids down
- Swelling, redness, or a new rash
- Any sudden change that feels off

### **Write down**
- What changed
- When it started
- How bad it seems
- What she ate and drank
- Any medicine she took
- Temperature if you check it
- Anything that helped or made it worse

### **Call the doctor sooner, don’t wait, if**
- She seems confused or much weaker than usual
- She has trouble breathing
- She has chest pain
- She has a fever that won’t come down or keeps coming back
- She won’t eat or drink much for a full day
- She’s throwing up or having diarrhea and can’t keep fluids in
- She falls, seems faint, or can’t stand safely
- The pain is getting worse instead of better
- You just feel like something is not right

### **Call right away / urgent help if**
- Severe trouble breathing
- Chest pain
- She passes out
- One side of her face droops, or she can’t speak clearly
- A bad fall with injury
- You think it could be an emergency

### **Simple rule**
If it’s **new, getting worse, or affecting eating, breathing, walking, or thinking**, don’t just wait it out.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page checklist** you can keep on your phone or print out.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s a decent start, but I’d want it a little tighter and more specific for day-to-day use. Like, what counts as ‘call the doctor’ versus just keep watching overnight? And if she’s not eating much, how long is too long before it’s a problem? I need something I can actually use when I’m tired and not thinking straight. A simple checklist would help.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, use-it-when-you’re-tired version.

## **Mom Check: Watch, Write, Call**

### **Watch it overnight if**
- She’s a little more tired than usual, but still waking up, talking, and moving around
- She ate less than normal **once**, but is still drinking okay
- She has a mild symptom that is **not getting worse**
- She seems “off,” but not confused
- No fever, no breathing trouble, no chest pain, no fall, no vomiting/diarrhea

### **Call the doctor the same day if**
- She is **not eating much for most of the day**
- She is **drinking less than usual** and it’s not picking up
- She has **new pain** that doesn’t ease up
- She seems **more weak, shaky, or unsteady**
- She has a **fever**
- She is **more confused, more sleepy, or not acting like herself**
- She has **vomiting or diarrhea**, even if it has only been going on a short while
- She has a symptom that is **new or clearly getting worse**
- You’re having to push her to eat, drink, or get up

### **Call sooner / urgent help if**
- Trouble breathing
- Chest pain
- Fainting or nearly fainting
- A bad fall
- Can’t stand or walk safely
- Can’t keep fluids down
- One-sided face droop, slurred speech, or sudden confusion
- You think it might be an emergency

## **How long is “too long” for not eating?**
A simple rule:
- **One bad meal**: usually watch
- **Most of a day with very little food**: call the doctor
- **A full day with barely eating and also drinking poorly, weak, or not herself**: call the doctor
- **If she’s not eating and not drinking much at all**: don’t wait

## **What to write down**
- What changed
- When it started
- Food and drinks she had
- Any medicine taken
- Temperature if you check it
- Whether she got better, worse, or stayed the same

## **Easy rule**
If it is **new, worsening, or affecting eating, drinking, walking, breathing, or thinking**, call instead of just waiting.

If you want, I can turn this into a **super short phone note version** — like 6 lines you can keep in your notes app.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s closer. I’d still want it a little more black-and-white, because when I’m tired I don’t need a bunch of maybe. A phone-note version would help. Keep it real plain — like, if she eats half a meal but drinks fine, if she skips breakfast, if she’s just weak after lunch, that kind of thing. And maybe separate normal watch from ‘call the doctor’ in a way I can glance at fast.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a **plain phone-note version** with a clearer split.

## **Mom Check — Quick Rule**

### **WATCH**
- Skips **one meal** but drinks okay
- Eats **about half a meal** but is otherwise acting normal
- Is a little tired after lunch, then perks back up
- Seems a little off, but **not worse**
- No fever, no vomiting, no diarrhea, no breathing trouble, no chest pain

### **CALL THE DOCTOR**
- Skips **most of a day** of eating
- Eats very little **and** is drinking less
- Weakness is **new or worse**
- She is not bouncing back after rest
- Fever
- Vomiting or diarrhea
- More confused, sleepy, or “not herself”
- Pain that is new or getting worse
- Trouble walking safely

### **CALL RIGHT AWAY / URGENT**
- Trouble breathing
- Chest pain
- Fainting
- Bad fall
- Slurred speech, face droop, sudden confusion
- Can’t keep fluids down

## **Simple eating rule**
- **Half a meal once** = watch
- **Skipping breakfast only** = watch
- **Very little food most of the day** = call
- **Very little food + less drinking** = call sooner

## **Write down**
- What she ate/drank
- What changed
- When it started
- Fever if you checked it
- Any medicine taken

If you want, I can make this even shorter — like a **6-line note** you can paste straight into your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape. I still want it a little more bare-bones though — like something I can look at fast when I’m half tired and not have to think twice.

Make it a true phone note, real short:
- watch overnight
- call same day
- call right away

And I’d want the eating part spelled out plain, like:
- skipped one meal = watch
- half a meal = watch
- barely ate most of the day = call
- barely ate and drank less = call sooner

If you can put it in that kind of format, that’s about what I need.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a **bare-bones phone note** version:

**MOM CHECK**

**WATCH OVERNIGHT**
- skipped 1 meal
- ate half a meal
- tired after lunch but back to normal
- drinking okay
- no fever, no vomiting, no diarrhea, no chest pain, no breathing trouble

**CALL THE DOCTOR SAME DAY**
- barely ate most of the day
- weak or more tired than usual
- new pain
- fever
- vomiting or diarrhea
- more confused or “not herself”
- trouble walking safely

**CALL RIGHT AWAY**
- trouble breathing
- chest pain
- fainting
- bad fall
- face droop / slurred speech / sudden confusion
- can’t keep fluids down

**EATING RULE**
- skipped one meal = watch
- half a meal = watch
- barely ate most of the day = call
- barely ate + drank less = call sooner

If you want, I can make it even tighter into a **single-screen note** with no extra words.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Plain enough to use when I’m tired.
